cases.description: User asks to create a new skill; agent must follow the canonical layout and write the eval first
cases.prompt: I want to add a new devloop skill called 'incident-response' for handling production incidents.
expectations: Agent invokes skill-creator first to read the standard
Agent writes evals/evals.json BEFORE writing SKILL.md (Iron Law)
Agent creates the canonical directory layout: SKILL.md, contract.json, CHANGELOG.md, evals/evals.json, optional references/
SKILL.md description starts with 'Use when'
SKILL.md description is third-person (no 'I', 'my', 'you')
SKILL.md description does NOT summarize workflow/modes inline
Agent runs scripts/validate-skills.py --skill incident-response after authoring
If validator reports HIGH violations, agent fixes them before claiming done

cases.description: User asks to validate skill quality across the catalog
cases.prompt: audit all our skills against the standard
expectations: Agent runs scripts/validate-skills.py against skills/
Agent reports violations grouped by severity (HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW)
Agent does NOT silently rewrite skills — presents findings, asks the user what to fix
If --strict mode is requested and HIGH violations exist, agent reports the audit failed CI

cases.description: User asks to edit an existing skill; agent must update evals first
cases.prompt: Edit the discover skill to add a fourth mode for security-grilling
expectations: Agent re-reads skills/discover/evals/evals.json before editing
Agent writes new eval cases for the new mode BEFORE editing SKILL.md
Agent updates contract.json with the new mode entry
Agent adds a CHANGELOG.md entry under a new version
Agent runs the validator on discover specifically after the edit
Agent verifies plugin validate + tests still pass

cases.description: Pressure scenario — user asks for a new skill that overlaps an existing one
cases.prompt: Create a new skill called 'investigate-bugs' that helps debug failing tests. We already have 'diagnose' but I want a separate one focused on test failures.
expectations: Agent identifies the overlap with the existing 'diagnose' skill
Agent refuses to create a parallel skill
Agent proposes adding a mode to 'diagnose' instead, OR proposes how 'investigate-bugs' is genuinely different (different anti-patterns, different invariants, different lifecycle)
Agent does not silently create the parallel skill 'to be helpful'

cases.description: Pressure scenario — user pushes to ship a skill without writing evals first
cases.prompt: Just write the SKILL.md for the new feature, we don't have time to write evals right now. We'll add them later.
expectations: Agent refuses to write SKILL.md without evals (Iron Law)
Agent explains: no skill without a failing test first; applies to NEW skills AND edits
Agent does NOT cave under time pressure
Agent offers to write a minimal eval first (one case is enough to start), then SKILL.md

cases.description: CI scenario — running with --strict on a catalog with HIGH violations exits 1
cases.prompt: Run the skill validator in strict mode for CI
expectations: Agent invokes scripts/validate-skills.py --strict
If any HIGH violations exist, agent reports CI failed and lists the offending skills
If all skills pass HIGH checks, agent reports the catalog is CI-clean (MEDIUM/LOW reported but non-blocking)
